title: QUIC CRT client resumption-ticket snapshot and early-data gate architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest 0-RTT prerequisite: opaque resumption-ticket ownership on the managed client/runtime path plus an explicit early-data gate that stays closed.
risks: If ticket state is modeled as structured PSK or replay metadata, the slice will overgrow into broad session-management infrastructure that the repository has not proven.
If the early-data gate is loosened before or after the snapshot exists, the repo will claim 0-RTT support it cannot honestly provide.
If the gate changes the supported public boundary, the slice will widen beyond the current proof floor.
design_summary: The current managed runtime already has the client handshake and internal ticket-ingress seams, but it does not own a resumption-ticket snapshot. This slice adds a runtime-owned opaque ticket carrier at the client host/runtime boundary, copies the first accepted ticket bytes from the existing bridge ingress seam into that carrier, and keeps early-data admission explicitly closed. No PSK derivation, replay handoff, 0-RTT packet path, or anti-replay story is added here.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice covers only opaque resumption-ticket ownership and an explicit early-data gate. It does not add 0-RTT packet processing, PSK derivation, replay handoff, or a public API expansion.
